questions.stem: An infant presents with the constellation of findings shown: mousy body odor, microcephaly, developmental delay, dry skin/eczema, and elevated serum phenylalanine. What is the most likely diagnosis?

explanation.summary: Phenylalanine hydroxylase deficiency causes phenylalanine accumulation, producing the classic mousy/musty odor, intellectual disability, microcephaly, seizures, eczema, and hypopigmentation. Management is dietary phenylalanine restriction.
explanation.whyCorrect: Phenylalanine hydroxylase deficiency causes phenylalanine accumulation, producing the classic mousy/musty odor, intellectual disability, microcephaly, seizures, eczema, and hypopigmentation. Management is dietary phenylalanine restriction.
